question.content: For the rotor-translation setup at the shown instant, compute the required force vector corresponding to the point-mass acceleration result. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Build the pseudo-3D rotor geometry and align inertial and translating frames.
step_1.key_point: x1, y1, z1 stay parallel to x, y, z at the instant.

step_3.content: Represent blade-end position by r on the rotor disk with angle theta and local thetahat direction.
step_3.key_point: Use theta measured from x1 in the rotor plane.

step_5.content: Set theta=90 and combine body translation with rotational tangential speed at A.

step_7.content: Acceleration equals translation acceleration plus centripetal term at theta=90.
